meta.objective: Audit every transition in World Models, Generative Models, RL & Control, and Embodied AI for the same disjointed prerequisite, motivation, and next-use problems, repair all material gaps, and independently verify the result.

tasks.title: Audit cross-course curriculum continuity
tasks.objective: Semantically inspect every home-to-introduction and adjacent-lesson transition in World Models, Generative Models, RL & Control, and Embodied AI, then produce an exhaustive severity-ranked continuity record.
acceptanceCriteria: All 138 transitions across the four non-LLM courses are enumerated and checked for prerequisite activation, why the next topic follows, next-use specificity, and unexplained-term leakage.
Every section boundary is explicitly classified as direct reuse, extension, synthesis, or an honestly labeled new chapter thread.
Every partial or failed seam has concrete reader-dossier evidence and a scoped repair recommendation; passing seams are accounted for rather than sampled.
A maintained regression contract and architecture evidence make the complete audit reproducible.
tasks.details: Use canonical reader snapshots and course manifests to inspect all four homes and 138 released lessons. Add the smallest durable audit record and structural regression coverage needed to prove population completeness; do not repair learner content until the audit is committed.

validationCommands.command: NODE_PATH="/Users/dt/Documents/LLMs from scratch/node_modules" node --test tests/cross-course-continuity.test.mjs tests/course-page-reader-snapshot.test.mjs tests/curriculum-architecture.test.mjs tests/documentation.test.mjs
validationCommands.evidence: 22/22 focused audit, reader-snapshot, architecture, and documentation tests passed

acceptanceCheckResults.criterion: All 138 transitions across the four non-LLM courses are enumerated and checked
acceptanceCheckResults.details: Exact populations are World Models 46, Generative 30, RL 32, and Embodied 30; every seam received semantic review.

acceptanceCheckResults.criterion: Every section boundary is explicitly classified
acceptanceCheckResults.details: All 31 course-home, track, and World advanced-branch entries are classified as direct reuse, extension, synthesis, or new chapter thread.

acceptanceCheckResults.criterion: Every partial or failed seam has evidence and a scoped repair
acceptanceCheckResults.details: The architecture record accounts for 90 pass, 48 partial, 0 fail and gives reader-level evidence plus a repair for all 48 partials.

acceptanceCheckResults.criterion: Maintained regression and architecture evidence make the audit reproducible
acceptanceCheckResults.details: A focused test derives the handoff population from canonical reader snapshots and requires maintained evidence for each finding.

commit.message: Task T001: Audit cross-course curriculum continuity

tasks.title: Repair cross-course continuity gaps
tasks.objective: Repair every material discontinuity found by T001 while preserving course IDs, order, assessment contracts, and beginner-first cumulative learning.
acceptanceCriteria: Every high- or medium-severity seam from the audit receives a coherent source-level repair or an explicit evidence-backed no-change disposition.
Repaired pages state prior knowledge, why the concept matters in the larger system, and where it will be reused before relying on new notation or jargon.
Related objectives, guides, examples, interactions, assessments, handoffs, manifests, and documentation agree after each repair.
Focused course, reader-snapshot, objective-coverage, and continuity tests pass without changing lesson IDs, order, routes, or persistence keys.
tasks.details: Implement the smallest coherent fixes identified by the exhaustive audit, add focused semantic regression assertions, update objective review records when learning promises change, and commit only complete cross-course work.

validationCommands.command: NODE_PATH="/Users/dt/Documents/LLMs from scratch/node_modules" node --test focused continuity/reader/curriculum/objective/documentation suites

validationCommands.evidence: Shared-core chooser, branch page, and capstone have no horizontal overflow or console errors; buttons are at least 83px high; capstone is a distinct synthesis.

acceptanceCheckResults.criterion: Every high- or medium-severity seam receives a coherent source-level repair or disposition
acceptanceCheckResults.details: All 36 high/medium findings and all 12 low findings have explicit learner-facing continuity bridges.

acceptanceCheckResults.criterion: Repaired pages state prior knowledge, system purpose, and reuse before new jargon
acceptanceCheckResults.details: The Where this chapter begins context now renders destination-specific bridges before vocabulary and deep mechanism prose.

acceptanceCheckResults.criterion: Related objectives, guides, examples, interactions, assessments, handoffs, manifests, and documentation agree
acceptanceCheckResults.details: Visible objectives and lesson mechanics were intentionally unchanged; renderer, reader dossiers, four-way relationship labels, World branch navigation, architecture evidence, and regression tests now agree.

acceptanceCheckResults.criterion: Focused tests pass without changing stable curriculum identity
acceptanceCheckResults.details: 44 focused tests plus lint and browser QA passed; no lesson ID, order, route, visible objective, or persistence key changed.

commit.message: Task T002: Repair cross-course curriculum continuity

tasks.title: Independently grade and verify cross-course repairs
tasks.objective: Prove the repaired transitions and affected pages satisfy their exact learning contracts and preserve the complete repository regression suite.
acceptanceCriteria: Independent graders pass every changed objective and complete affected page with zero unresolved partial or failed dimensions.
Independent continuity review accounts for all 138 audited transitions and confirms every material finding is resolved.
Current reader hashes, course fingerprints, whole-page grade records, and dated review evidence match the independently reviewed source.
npm run lint and npm test pass in the isolated worktree with no unrelated files included.
tasks.details: Run blind semantic grading after authoring, remediate until no partial or fail remains, refresh only affected evidence records, then run the required full repository checks and commit the verified result.

validationCommands.command: blind semantic review of all 138 current handoffs plus fresh regrade of three remediations
validationCommands.evidence: Final result 138 pass, 0 partial, 0 fail; no affected destination page was internally contradictory

validationCommands.command: npm run verify:grades and focused continuity/reader/objective/architecture/documentation tests
validationCommands.evidence: All 187 grade rows verified and 42/42 focused tests passed
Static export, Pages URLs, artifacts, visuals, grade evidence, and all 219 tests passed

acceptanceCheckResults.criterion: Independent graders pass every changed objective and complete affected page
acceptanceCheckResults.details: No visible objective changed; the grader found no internal contradiction in affected destination pages and the existing 408/408 objective gate remains closed.

acceptanceCheckResults.criterion: Independent continuity review accounts for all 138 transitions
acceptanceCheckResults.details: The blind grader independently reconstructed all 138 handoffs, surfaced three residual seams, and passed all three after remediation for a final 138/138 result.

acceptanceCheckResults.criterion: Reader hashes, source fingerprints, grades, and dated review evidence match current source
acceptanceCheckResults.details: All 187 grade rows verify against current dossiers and fingerprints; the new continuity registry is included in future fingerprint invalidation.

acceptanceCheckResults.criterion: Required full repository checks pass in the isolated worktree
acceptanceCheckResults.details: Lint, the complete static-export and artifact workflow, all 219 regression tests, and whitespace checks passed.
